action.reasoning: Observation: The baseline heuristic (local greedy utility/distance with visited penalty) scored only 0.0145 exploration_score with 1.3% success. Analysis: It likely gets stuck in local optima because it only looks one step ahead. We have the full exploration graph in obs['nodes'], so we can run global planning (Dijkstra) to pick the best destination node anywhere on the graph and move one step along the shortest path. This should dramatically improve success rate. Decision: Replace predict.py with a graph-based heuristic that scores every node by utility/(path_distance+1) and traces back the first step via Dijkstra. Keep dummy checkpoint. Run a quick smoke test on a training map to verify no crashes, then immediately submit evaluation to measure the new score and iteration time.
